title: Thread context: ancestors oldest-first, descendants oldest-first, no infinite loop
description:
Reconstruct a thread. ancestors: walk in_reply_to chain upward from the requested post to the root, then REVERSE for oldest-first order. descendants: find all posts with in_reply_to pointing to this post or its descendants (BFS or DFS), sort oldest-first. Handle mix of posts and remote_posts tables. Enforce depth limit to prevent pathological threads.

THIS IS HISTORICALLY BUGGY. Test extensively.

Agent workflow: Spawn a research subagent to test /api/v1/statuses/:id/context against mastodon.social for a 5+ level nested thread and document the exact ordering as oracle. Spawn a testing subagent to construct adversarial test cases (circular references, very deep threads, mixed local+remote).

Test discipline:
- 3-level thread A→B→C: context for B has ancestors=[A], descendants=[C]
- Context for A: ancestors=[], descendants=[B, C]
- Context for C: ancestors=[A, B], descendants=[]
- Mixed local+remote thread: ancestors includes remote posts
- Circular reference (shouldn't happen but protect): depth limit breaks loop
- 50-level deep thread: depth limit triggers at some reasonable depth

Defensive programming:
- Depth limit: stop ancestor walk at depth 40 (prevents infinite loop from circular refs)
- descendants: BFS with visited set to prevent revisiting nodes
- ancestors ordering: collect as list while walking up, then reverse — don't sort by ID (IDs across local+remote may not be in the same namespace)
acceptance_criteria: Ancestors are oldest-first (root first); descendants are oldest-first; circular references don't infinite-loop; 50-deep thread handled within limit; matches mastodon.social oracle
